steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure on each side of the blank
steps.content: The phrase before the blank is a prepositional phrase (‘with ______’), and the phrase after the blank is an independent clause (‘another research team...’).

steps.title: Apply the rule for connecting a prepositional phrase and an independent clause with a conjunctive adverb
steps.content: A semicolon connects an independent clause to a conjunctive adverb like ‘for instance’ before it and uses a comma after the adverb. Choice B places the semicolon before ‘for instance’ and the comma after, following the rule. The other three either reverse the punctuation (A), omit the semicolon entirely (C), or use commas incorrectly for a conjunctive adverb (D). That matches choice B — B is correct.
